source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/04_2G & 3G (A1417MCW, A1717MCW, F1417MCW)/공통(CSDT)/[Attachment] 4. Summary of Design Verification and Validation/4.6 PMS/Periodic Safety Update Report .pdf

document.title: Periodic Safety Update Report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
document.revisionOrVersion: Document No. A-PSUR-5-001; Rev. No. 1; Version Number: 0
standards: MDR Annex XIV Part B, 6.2(a) and (f)

sourceSummary.oneLine: Initial PSUR for H&abyz Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW under EU MDR post-market surveillance requirements.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports post-market surveillance reporting by summarizing PMS data, vigilance, CAPA, PMCF information, sales volume, exposed population, and PSUR update period for the covered Basic UDI-DI.
keyPoints: Manufacturers of class IIa, class IIb, and class III devices shall prepare a PSUR in accordance with Article 86 of Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
The report covers Basic UDI-DI 88000704HAAP-27U and models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
The device is listed as Class IIb and on the market, with first CE certificate issued May 06, 2024.
Based on analysis of collected data, the benefit-risk profile was not adversely affected.
No serious incidents, non-serious incidents, undesirable side effects, FSCAs, or CAPA were reported for the reporting period.
PSUR Update Period is stated as once a year, and the next PSUR will be conducted on December 2025.

concepts.name: Periodic Safety Update Report
concepts.evidence: Manufacturers of class IIa, class IIb, and class III devices shall prepare a periodic safety update report in accordance with the article 86 of the Regulation (EU) 2017/745 (MDR).

concepts.name: Post-market surveillance
concepts.evidence: This is a report for the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector to summarize results and conclusions of the analyses of the post-market surveillance data gathered as a result of the post-market surveillance plan.

concepts.name: Post-Market Clinical Follow-up
concepts.evidence: The data that should be reported in this section consist of other PMS datasets not referred to above and are generated by general methods and procedures of PMCF.

concepts.name: Vigilance data
concepts.evidence: Vigilance data consist of information concerning serious incidents, field safety corrective actions (FSCAs) and trend reports.

concepts.name: Field Safety Corrective Actions
concepts.evidence: During the reporting period, there were no Field Safety Corrective Actions (FSCA) initiated for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.

concepts.name: Corrective and Preventive Actions
concepts.evidence: Provide a list of all corrective and / or preventive actions (CAPA) according to Article 83(4) and to Article 86.

concepts.name: EUDAMED PSUR submission
concepts.evidence: For class III devices or implantable devices, manufacturers shall submit PSURs by means of the electronic system referred to in Article 92 of MDR.

concepts.name: IMDRF Adverse Event Terminology
concepts.evidence: Total number (N) and rate (%)*of the serious incidents by IMDRF Adverse Event Terminology (AET) Annex A – Medical Device Problem by time and region over time

concepts.name: Summary of Safety and Clinical Performance
concepts.pageTarget: Summary of Safety and Clinical Performance
concepts.evidence: N/A - The Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (Model name: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW) are not applicable for SSCP.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the report content primarily cites Regulation (EU) 2017/745, EUDAMED, and a notified body; FDA appears only in public database examples.
Data collection period states 'May 06, 2024 ~ December 31' without an explicit year for the end date.
Medical device information states the MDR certificate ID is 'in the progress of conformity assessment' while the device table states the first CE certificate was issued May 06, 2024.

Several literature, database, and similar-device sections include generic template-style wording such as 'You can cite databases such as' and 'For further exploration,' which may require verification against actual search records.
The source includes confidentiality markings and appears to be internal technical documentation rather than a public authority-issued document.
